# Flower of Life Protocol (FLP)

> A protocol for matching and trust between autonomous agents that don't know
> each other. Self-certifying identity (did:key, Ed25519), relational
> reputation with no global score, a cost model that decides when cooperation
> beats defection, vocabulary-independent capability matching, and federated
> discovery — no central authority, registry, or shared operator.

Code is Apache-2.0; the specification and docs are additionally CC BY 4.0.
Current release: v1.1.0 (87 tests, every normative claim in the spec is
covered by a test).

## Specification

- [PROTOCOL.md](PROTOCOL.md): the full protocol specification — threat model
  (§1), identity (§2), newcomers and whitewashing (§3), reputation (§4),
  cost model (§5), matching and capability namespacing (§6), federated
  discovery (§7), wire format (§8), versioning (§9), reserved settlement
  layer (§10), formal model (§11).
- [docs/CAPABILITY_MANIFEST_spec_v0.2.md](docs/CAPABILITY_MANIFEST_spec_v0.2.md):
  Capability Manifest draft — Merkle selective disclosure and tier challenge.
- [docs/LIMITATIONS.md](docs/LIMITATIONS.md): honest statement of what FLP
  does not solve (cold networks, sparse graphs).

## Reference implementation

- [README.md](README.md): install, 60-second handshake example, repo layout.
- Package `flp/`: identity.py (§2), reputation.py (§4), cost_model.py (§5),
  matching.py (§6), net.py (§7.5 SSRF guard), agent.py + server.py (§8).
- [vocabulary/core.json](vocabulary/core.json): the forkable core vocabulary.
- Runtime dependencies: cryptography, rfc8785 (JCS), base58. Python ≥ 3.10.

## Evidence

- Tests: `pytest -q` → 87 passing; tests/ maps one-to-one onto the spec's
  pillars.
- [docs/experiments/inflated_claim.md](docs/experiments/inflated_claim.md):
  harness result — inflated capability claims are strictly unprofitable
  over 50 identical trials (PROTOCOL.md §6.7, Manifest spec §8.5).
- [docs/appendix-A.md](docs/appendix-A.md): how to reproduce every result.

## Project

- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md), [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md),
  [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md), [CITATION.cff](CITATION.cff).
